Assignment due 30 June

AIDI drop-ins are Tuesdays 11-12 at  [https://qa-learning.webex.com/meet/AJames2](https://qa-learning.webex.com/meet/AJames2)

Artefact, can't just use an api or just prompt engineering.

Want to design and implement something.

Proof of concept NLP.

Youtubers

Kevin Stratvert, Josh Starmer, Andrej Karpathy

From <[https://web.webex.com/meeting?joinInfoOrigin=https%3A%2F%2Fqa-learning.webex.com&sessionCorrelationId=c806ec9e-019b-43ec-adc2-4afccfb797a5&correlationId=bc52f2c3-6c92-4d6c-8407-5a94edb1f7ae&mtuuid=15832f6c1c16480557405e3afcb71adb&preload=false](https://web.webex.com/meeting?joinInfoOrigin=https%3A%2F%2Fqa-learning.webex.com&sessionCorrelationId=c806ec9e-019b-43ec-adc2-4afccfb797a5&correlationId=bc52f2c3-6c92-4d6c-8407-5a94edb1f7ae&mtuuid=15832f6c1c16480557405e3afcb71adb&preload=false)>

[LLM Visualization](https://bbycroft.net/llm)

Recurrent neural networks

Long short term memory

RNN

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Vanishing gradient problem, RNN forgets stuff over time

[https://colah.github.io/posts/2015-08-Understanding-LSTMs/](https://colah.github.io/posts/2015-08-Understanding-LSTMs/)

LSTM uses gating mechanisms, hand over more information.

Not parallelisable, need to train each bit one at a time.

Attention, understanding relationship of words to each other

Bahdanau - LSTM + Attention since 2014

But issue was that it was still linear.

2017

10:55

Transformers explained; understanding the model behind BERT GPT-3 and T5

Any model needs to capture word order.

RNN, are bad at long text, since it forgets what happens at the beginning. Plus hard to train

You can parallelise transformers

Store data about order of word in the word itself rather than as part of the network

Attention. Looks at every word in the original sentence about a word.

By seeing lots of examples, it learns how words are related

Self-attention helps the neural network understand the meaning better.

BERT shows you can build model on unlabelled data.

[https://arxiv.org/pdf/1706.03762I](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1706.03762I)

Get rid of recurrent stuff and just uses attention.

[https://d2l.ai/chapter_recurrent-modern/lstm.html](https://d2l.ai/chapter_recurrent-modern/lstm.html)

Group Topics -> 1 Tokenisation 2 Word embeddings 3 Parallelisable & GPU 4 Multi-head attention 5 Feedforward neural networks 6 Positional Encoding (at a high-level!) 7 Common Crawl, Books3 & FineWeb Researching for 10 minutes, then we'll come back to report. Please pick a Speaker Looking for good images/diagrams/concepts that explain these things. Avoid heavy math or code for now :)

From <[https://web.webex.com/meeting?joinInfoOrigin=https%3A%2F%2Fqa-learning.webex.com&sessionCorrelationId=c806ec9e-019b-43ec-adc2-4afccfb797a5&correlationId=bc52f2c3-6c92-4d6c-8407-5a94edb1f7ae&mtuuid=15832f6c1c16480557405e3afcb71adb&preload=false](https://web.webex.com/meeting?joinInfoOrigin=https%3A%2F%2Fqa-learning.webex.com&sessionCorrelationId=c806ec9e-019b-43ec-adc2-4afccfb797a5&correlationId=bc52f2c3-6c92-4d6c-8407-5a94edb1f7ae&mtuuid=15832f6c1c16480557405e3afcb71adb&preload=false)>

Parallelisable & GPU 

From <[https://web.webex.com/meeting?joinInfoOrigin=https%3A%2F%2Fqa-learning.webex.com&sessionCorrelationId=c806ec9e-019b-43ec-adc2-4afccfb797a5&correlationId=bc52f2c3-6c92-4d6c-8407-5a94edb1f7ae&mtuuid=15832f6c1c16480557405e3afcb71adb&preload=false](https://web.webex.com/meeting?joinInfoOrigin=https%3A%2F%2Fqa-learning.webex.com&sessionCorrelationId=c806ec9e-019b-43ec-adc2-4afccfb797a5&correlationId=bc52f2c3-6c92-4d6c-8407-5a94edb1f7ae&mtuuid=15832f6c1c16480557405e3afcb71adb&preload=false)>

So the 512 dimension input is split into 8 heads, which can run in parralell on a GPU

Tokenise can explain why gpt can't do some stuff like count r's in strawberry. If you want to do something around that then ask gpt to write code to do it.

[https://platform.openai.com/tokenizer](https://platform.openai.com/tokenizer)

[Transformers (how LLMs work) explained visually | DL5](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wjZofJX0v4M)
